original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: Ministry of Health and Prevention, United Arab Emirates
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Practical Guideline for Management of Tobacco Dependence
original_registry_entry.population: People who use tobacco, recently stopped tobacco, do not currently use tobacco, or may be exposed to second-hand smoke.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Clinical and public-health tobacco-use identification, assessment, cessation support, and follow-up.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Direct UAE source for documenting tobacco status, exposure, quit readiness, prior attempts, barriers, clinician-discussed support, referral, and follow-up; medicine names, doses, and autonomous cessation instructions are not mapped.

exact_sections.heading: Who Can Use this Guide and Basic and Essential Facts
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports healthcare-provider use, tobacco-status identification and documentation, cessation support, and second-hand-smoke exposure context.

exact_sections.heading: The 5 As and Identification and Assessment of Tobacco Use Status
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports current and former tobacco status, product use, willingness and readiness to quit, previous attempts, motivation, confidence, barriers, and dependence assessment including smoking rate and time to first cigarette.

exact_sections.heading: Assist and Arrange follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented counselling, support, pharmacotherapy discussion, referral, and arranged follow-up without mapping medicine names or doses.

exact_sections.heading: Former tobacco users, relapse potential, and behavioural intervention summary
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting recent abstinence, remaining challenges, relapse risk, prior success, repeated intervention, and follow-up.

title: Practical Guideline for Management of Tobacco Dependence
publisher: Ministry of Health and Prevention, United Arab Emirates
population: People who use tobacco, recently stopped tobacco, do not currently use tobacco, or may be exposed to second-hand smoke.
intended_setting: Clinical and public-health tobacco-use identification, assessment, cessation support, and follow-up.
access_limitations: One or more PDF tables require structured extraction
Algorithm or figure references preserved as locators; diagram structure not interpreted
